channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: How a wrong Google search can compromise your data and bring law enforcement calling
item.description: Wrong Google searches can not only compromise your device and personal data. They can also bring law enforcement to your doorstep. Hackers are targeting Google searches.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Farewell to screen scratches? Advanced nanocoating technology could be coming to over a million devices
item.description: Optitune partners with a trillion-dollar tech company to introduce innovative Invisible Fingerprint nanocoating.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: The original two-year period for free Emergency SOS Satellite features on the iPhone has expired – but Apple hasn't started charging yet
item.description: We're now into the bonus year for iPhone 14 owners to access satellite features, but it's not clear what happens next.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Dude Perfect went from YouTube to raising $100M. Here’s how its new CEO wants to grow the brand
item.description: Over the past 15 years, sports and entertainment company Dude Perfect—which started as a YouTube channel where five best friends posted videos of trick shots in different sports—has grown into a media juggernaut with attendant cruises, toy lines, and TV deals.
item.dc:creator: Yasmin Gagne, Fast Company

item.title: What you need to know about brain-training games
item.description: Some 2.3 million U.S. adults over 65—more than 4%—have a diagnosis of dementia. But even without a diagnosis, a certain amount of cognitive decline is normal as age sets in.
item.dc:creator: The Conversation, Fast Company

item.title: New framework aims to keep AI safe in US critical infrastructure
item.description: Analyst reaction to Thursday’s release by the US Department of Homeland Security (DHS) of a framework designed to ensure safe and secure deployment of AI in critical infrastructure is decidedly mixed.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Go language evolving for future hardware, AI workloads
item.description: The Go programming language having just turned 15 years old on November 10, proponents now are planning to adapt the Go language to large multicore systems, the latest vector and matrix hardware instructions, and the needs of AI workloads.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft’s Patch Tuesday updates: Keeping up with the latest fixes
item.description: Long before Taco Tuesday became part of the pop-culture vernacular, Tuesdays were synonymous with security — and for anyone in the tech world, they still are.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Psaki: 'Disinformation space' on social media a 'core' issue behind Harris defeat
item.description: Former White House press secretary Jen Psaki is blaming Vice President Harris's loss to President-elect Trump in part on disinformation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Google’s Gemini app is now available on iPhones
item.description: Google has entered a new and more intense phase of the AI wars, introducing its own Google Gemini app for iPhones; now you can use Apple Intelligence, ChatGPT, Microsoft Copilot and Google Gemini on one device.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Alejandro Mayorkas says industry was "intensely engaged" in making the new DHS guidelines, and it's up to companies to adopt them. The post DHS unveils ‘practical’ AI responsibilities for critical infrastructure first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: AI is everywhere. How should California schools handle it? Teachers’ different approaches show its potential — and limits
item.description: 'We’re trying to teach the ethical use of these new tools that are just going to keep on growing,' one teacher says.
item.dc:creator: Kristen Taketa, The Mercury News

item.title: Why that Google Meet error might be a sneaky hacker trick
item.description: ClickFix, a new scam targeting computer users, is on the rise in the U.S. The scam prompts its targets to click on a link to fix a problem.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One Tech Tip: Replacing passwords with passkeys for an easier login experience
item.description: LONDON (AP) — If you're tired of memorizing passwords, then give passkeys a try. You might have noticed that many online services are now offering the option of using passkeys, a digital authentication method touted as an easier and more secure way to log in.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Chloe Fineman says Elon Musk made her ‘burst into tears’ during ‘SNL’ hosting gig
item.description: Chloe Fineman revealed that Elon Musk is the "SNL" host who made a cast member cry, after the SpaceX and Tesla honcho slammed the show for its post-election impression of him.
item.dc:creator: Jami Ganz, The Mercury News

item.title: IBM preps Power servers with AI support, new processor
item.description: IBM’s plans for its Power server family promise faster and more programmable AI capabilities along with a new, more powerful processor. Highlights of the 2025 roadmap include: IBM’s Spyre Accelerator for AI will be part of the Power processing systems.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Chinese hackers target Tibetan websites in malware attack, cybersecurity group says
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A hacking group that is believed to be Chinese state-sponsored has compromised two websites with ties to the Tibetan community in an attack meant to install malware on users' computers, according to findings released Wednesday by a private cybersecurity firm.
item.dc:creator: DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Bitcoin neared $90,000 in a new record high. What to know about crypto's post-election rally
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As money continues to pour into crypto following Donald Trump's reelection last week, bitcoin has climbed to yet another record high. The world's largest cryptocurrency topped $89,000 for the first time, briefly peaking at $89,995 early Tuesday, according to CoinDesk.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Elon Musk responds after 'SNL' star claims he made her cry
item.description: In a now-deleted TikTok post, "Saturday Night Live" star Chloe Fineman said that Elon Musk made her cry when he hosted the show.
item.dc:creator: Darren Sweeney, NewsNation

item.title: Japan's SoftBank returns to profit after gains at Vision Fund and other investments
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Japanese technology group SoftBank swung back to profitability in the July-September quarter, boosted by positive results in its Vision Fund investments. Tokyo-based SoftBank Group Corp.
item.dc:creator: YURI KAGEYAMA, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Why your AI models stumble before the finish line
item.description: In 2023, enterprises across industries invested heavily in generative AI proof of concepts (POCs), eager to explore the technology’s potential. Fast-forward to 2024, companies face a new challenge: moving AI initiatives from prototype to production.
item.dc:creator: Info World
